title: Me! Come! My Dazzled Face
content:
Me! Come! My dazzled face
    In such a shining place!

    Me! Hear! My foreign ear
    The sounds of welcome near!

    The saints shall meet
    Our bashful feet.

    My holiday shall be
    That they remember me;

    My paradise, the fame
    That they pronounce my name.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Emily Elizabeth Dickinson delivers a powerful performance in "Me! Come! My Dazzled Face"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
